message:
Implement type widening for mixed-type lists

Added type promotion system to handle heterogeneous Python lists:

1. NativeType.widen() - Type promotion function
   - Hierarchy: int < float < string < unknown
   - float can hold ints (widens int→float)
   - string is universal (everything has str())
   - Incompatible types fall back to unknown (PyObject)

2. List generation with type widening
   - Analyze ALL elements before choosing ArrayList type
   - Start with first element, widen to accommodate rest
   - Auto-cast when needed: int→float via @floatFromInt

3. Examples that now work:
   - [1, 2.5, 3] → ArrayList(f64) with int→float casts
   - [1, "hello"] → ArrayList(unknown) fallback
   - Pure types unchanged: [1,2,3] → ArrayList(i64)

Test results: 44 → 45 passing (+1 test)
Fixes mixed-type list compilation errors
